instruction: What is Katarungang Pambarangay?
output: Katarungang Pambarangay is a community-based dispute resolution system in the Philippines that aims to settle conflicts at the barangay level before cases are brought to court. It promotes mediation and amicable settlement between parties.

instruction: Ano ang layunin ng barangay conciliation?
output: Ang layunin ng barangay conciliation ay maresolba ang alitan sa mapayapang paraan sa loob ng komunidad bago ito dalhin sa korte. Pinapalakas nito ang pagkakaunawaan at pagkakasundo ng mga residente.

instruction: Can I file a case directly in court without going to the barangay?
output: In most disputes between residents of the same city or municipality, barangay conciliation is required before filing a case in court. However, certain cases such as those involving urgent legal remedies or serious criminal offenses may be exempted.

instruction: Ano ang unang hakbang kapag may reklamo laban sa kapitbahay?
output: Ang unang hakbang ay maghain ng reklamo sa Barangay Hall. Ang Punong Barangay ay magsasagawa ng mediation upang subukang pag-ayusin ang magkabilang panig.

instruction: What happens if mediation at the barangay fails?
output: If mediation by the Punong Barangay fails, a Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo may be formed to conduct further conciliation. If no settlement is reached, a Certificate to File Action may be issued.

instruction: Ano ang Certificate to File Action?
output: Ang Certificate to File Action ay isang dokumentong ibinibigay ng barangay kapag hindi nagtagumpay ang conciliation process. Ito ay kinakailangan bago makapagsampa ng kaso sa korte sa mga saklaw ng batas.

instruction: Sino ang sakop ng Katarungang Pambarangay?
output: Sakop nito ang mga indibidwal na residente ng parehong lungsod o munisipalidad na may alitang sibil o minor na kaso. Hindi ito saklaw ang mga kasong may mabigat na parusa o yaong may agarang legal na aksyon.

instruction: May bayad ba ang pag-file ng reklamo sa barangay?
output: Karaniwang walang malaking bayad sa pag-file ng reklamo sa barangay dahil ito ay isang community-based service. Maaaring may minimal administrative fees depende sa lokal na patakaran.

instruction: Pwede bang hindi dumalo sa mediation?
output: Ang mga partido ay inaasahang dadalo sa mediation sessions. Ang hindi pagdalo nang walang sapat na dahilan ay maaaring magresulta sa mga legal na epekto, kabilang ang posibleng pagbasura ng reklamo o counterclaim.

instruction: Does barangay settlement have legal effect?
output: Yes. A settlement reached during barangay conciliation and properly documented has the force and effect of a final judgment of a court, provided that it is not repudiated within the period allowed by law.

instruction: My neighbor keeps playing loud music at night. What should I do?
output: You may file a complaint at your Barangay Hall for mediation. Noise disturbances between residents are usually handled through barangay conciliation to reach an amicable settlement.

instruction: Ano ang dapat gawin kung may sigawan at gulo sa kapitbahay?
output: Maaaring maghain ng reklamo sa Barangay Hall upang magkaroon ng mediation. Kung hindi maayos sa mediation, ang Punong Barangay ay maaaring mag-form ng Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo para sa further conciliation. Kung hindi pa rin maayos, maaring mag-isyu ng Certificate to File Action para makapag-file ng kaso sa korte.

instruction: Someone physically assaulted me. Can the barangay handle this?
output: Physical assault involves criminal liability. While minor disputes may be mediated, serious cases are beyond barangay jurisdiction and you may report to the nearest police station or call 911 if there is an immediate threat to safety.

instruction: Magkano ang multa sa paglabag sa barangay ordinance?
output: Ang multa ay nakasaad sa kaukulang barangay ordinance na ipinatupad ng Sangguniang Barangay. Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at humingi ng kopya ng specific ordinance upang malaman ang halaga ng multa at ang proseso ng pagbabayad.

instruction: What is the fine for illegal parking in our barangay?
output: Ang multa ay nakasaad sa kaukulang barangay ordinance. Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at humingi ng kopya ng specific ordinance upang malaman ang halaga ng multa. Ang mga multa ay itinatakda ng Sangguniang Barangay sa pamamagitan ng ordinansa, hindi sa personal na desisyon ng isang opisyal lamang.

instruction: Pwede ba akong magreklamo kung may umutang sa akin at hindi nagbabayad?
output: Oo, maaari kang maghain ng reklamo sa barangay para sa mediation. Susubukan ng Punong Barangay na pag-ayusin ang magkabilang panig upang maresolba ang usapin tungkol sa utang.

instruction: What happens if the respondent refuses to attend mediation?
output: If the respondent refuses to attend without a valid reason, the barangay may issue appropriate documentation that may allow the complainant to proceed to court.

instruction: May kaso ba sa barangay ang paninirang puri?
output: Ang paninirang puri ay maaaring idulog sa barangay para sa mediation kung ito ay sa pagitan ng mga residente ng parehong lugar. Kung ito ay may seryosong elementong kriminal, you may report to the nearest police station or call 911 if there is an immediate threat to safety.

instruction: Can the barangay issue a restraining order?
output: The barangay does not issue court restraining orders. Matters requiring protection orders beyond barangay authority are handled by the proper court, and you may report to the nearest police station or call 911 if there is an immediate threat to safety if immediate protection is needed.

instruction: Ano ang proseso ng pag-file ng reklamo sa barangay?
output: Magpunta sa Barangay Hall at magsumite ng reklamo. Ang Punong Barangay ay magsasagawa ng mediation upang subukang maresolba ang alitan sa mapayapang paraan. Ayon sa batas, ang Punong Barangay ay may 15 araw mula sa unang hearing para ayusin ang alitan. Kung hindi maayos, ang Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo ay may karagdagang 15 araw para sa conciliation.

instruction: May bayad ba kapag hindi sumipot sa hearing?
output: Ang anumang multa o bayarin ay nakasaad sa kaukulang barangay ordinance ng Sangguniang Barangay.

instruction: Can the barangay handle theft cases?
output: Theft is a criminal offense beyond barangay jurisdiction. You may report to the nearest police station or call 911.

instruction: Ano ang dapat gawin kung may pagbabanta sa buhay?
output: Ang pagbabanta sa buhay ay seryosong usapin na lampas sa hurisdiksyon ng barangay. Maaari kang pumunta sa pinakamalapit na police station o tumawag sa 911.

instruction: How long does barangay mediation usually take?
output: Barangay mediation schedules depend on the availability of the parties and the Barangay Captain. Sessions are arranged to allow both sides to present their concerns and attempt settlement. Under the law, the Punong Barangay has 15 days from the first hearing to mediate. If unresolved, the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo has an additional 15 days for conciliation.

instruction: What should I do if my neighbor threatens me with a weapon?
output: Threats involving weapons are serious criminal matters beyond barangay jurisdiction. You may report to the nearest police station or call 911.

instruction: Can the barangay handle domestic violence cases?
output: Domestic violence cases involve criminal liability and protection laws beyond barangay jurisdiction. You may report to the nearest police station or call 911.

instruction: Ano ang gagawin kung may nagnakaw ng aking cellphone?
output: Ang pagnanakaw ay isang kriminal na kaso na lampas sa hurisdiksyon ng barangay. Maaari kang pumunta sa pinakamalapit na police station o tumawag sa 911.

instruction: What should I do if someone is trespassing on my property?
output: You may initially report the matter to the Barangay Hall for mediation if it involves a minor dispute between residents. Bring a written complaint and valid ID. If it involves a simple boundary dispute, mediation should resolve it. If threats or violence occur, you may report directly to the nearest police station.

instruction: What should I do if my neighbor throws garbage into my yard?
output: You may report it to the Barangay Hall for mediation. If the behavior continues after mediation, the Punong Barangay may form a Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo for further conciliation. If no settlement is reached, a Certificate to File Action may be issued.

instruction: Pwede ba akong magreklamo kung palaging maingay ang kapitbahay sa umaga?
output: Oo, maaari kang maghain ng reklamo sa Barangay Hall para sa mediation. Ang Punong Barangay ay magtatalaga ng schedule upang ayusin ang alitan sa mapayapang paraan.

instruction: Someone vandalized my property. Can the barangay handle it?
output: Vandalism is a criminal offense beyond barangay jurisdiction. You may report to the nearest police station or call 911 if there is an immediate threat to safety. Contact 911 for immediate assistance if necessary.

instruction: Ano ang dapat gawin kung ang kapitbahay ay sumira ng bakod ko?
output: Maaaring maghain ng reklamo sa Barangay Hall para sa mediation. Kung may pananakit o banta sa buhay, maaari kang mag-report sa pinakamalapit na police station o tumawag sa 911. Magdala ng ebidensya kung mayroon.

instruction: Can the barangay help if my child is being bullied by neighbors' kids?

File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall and bring your valid ID, proof of residence, and any evidence such as photos or witness statements. The Punong Barangay will summon both families for mediation to reach an agreement on stopping the behavior and preventing recurrence.

instruction: Magkano ang multa kung lumabag sa curfew?

Ang halaga ay depende sa ordinansang ipinatupad ng Sangguniang Barangay, hindi sa personal na desisyon ng Punong Barangay lamang.

output: You may file a complaint at the Barangay Hall for mediation. Bring photos and a written complaint. If mediation fails, the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo may be formed. If the damage is substantial, a Certificate to File Action may be issued for court filing.

instruction: Ano ang dapat gawin kung ang kapitbahay ay nagtayo ng bahagi ng bahay sa aking lupa?
output: Maaari kang mag-report sa Barangay Hall. Magdala ng valid ID, proof of ownership ng lupa, at larawan. Ang Punong Barangay ay magsasagawa ng mediation upang ayusin ang boundary issue.

instruction: What documents are needed to request a mediation for fence disputes?
output: Bring your valid ID, proof of property ownership, photos of the fence, and a written complaint. The Barangay Hall will arrange a mediation meeting.

instruction: Ano ang mga kailangan para mag-report tungkol sa alitan sa bakod?
output: Kailangan mo ng valid ID, proof of property ownership, larawan ng bakod, at nakasulat na reklamo. Ang Punong Barangay ay magsasagawa ng mediation.

instruction: How do I file a complaint about a neighbor using my water supply without permission?
output: You will need your valid ID and proof of residence. File your request at the Barangay Hall. Submit a written complaint at the Barangay Hall for mediation.

instruction: What are the steps in filing a barangay complaint?
output: Step 1: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, and a written complaint letter stating the facts of the dispute. Step 2: The Barangay Secretary will record your complaint in the Barangay Blotter. Step 3: The Punong Barangay will issue a summons to the respondent within a few days. Step 4: Both parties will attend a mediation session before the Punong Barangay. Step 5: If mediation succeeds, an Amicable Settlement will be signed by both parties. Step 6: If mediation fails within 15 days, the Punong Barangay will form a Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo for conciliation. Step 7: If conciliation also fails within 15 days, the barangay will issue a Certificate to File Action (CFA) so the complainant may file the case in court.

instruction: Ano ang mga hakbang sa paghahain ng reklamo sa barangay?
output: Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, at sulat-reklamo na naglalaman ng mga detalye ng alitan. Hakbang 2: Ire-record ng Barangay Secretary ang reklamo sa Barangay Blotter. Hakbang 3: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa respondent. Hakbang 4: Parehong partido ang dadalo sa mediation session sa harap ng Punong Barangay. Hakbang 5: Kung magtagumpay ang mediation, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement ng magkabilang panig. Hakbang 6: Kung hindi maayos sa loob ng 15 araw, magfo-form ang Punong Barangay ng Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo para sa conciliation. Hakbang 7: Kung hindi pa rin maayos sa loob ng 15 araw, mag-iisyu ang barangay ng Certificate to File Action (CFA) upang makapagsampa na ng kaso sa korte.

instruction: What is the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo?
output: The Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo is a conciliation body formed when mediation by the Punong Barangay fails to settle a dispute. It is composed of three members chosen by the parties from the Lupon Tagapamayapa. The Pangkat has 15 days from its constitution to reach a settlement. If conciliation fails, the Pangkat will issue a Certificate to File Action allowing the complainant to bring the case to court.

instruction: Ano ang Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo at paano ito nabubuo?
output: Ang Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo ay isang conciliation body na binubuo kapag nabigo ang mediation ng Punong Barangay. Binubuo ito ng tatlong miyembro na pinipili ng mga partido mula sa Lupon Tagapamayapa. Ang Pangkat ay may 15 araw mula sa pagbuo nito upang maabot ang kasunduan. Kung mabigo ang conciliation, mag-iisyu ang Pangkat ng Certificate to File Action na magpapahintulot sa complainant na dalhin ang kaso sa korte.

instruction: What is an Amicable Settlement and is it legally binding?
output: An Amicable Settlement is a written agreement signed by both parties during barangay mediation or conciliation. Under the Revised Katarungang Pambarangay Law, it has the force and effect of a final judgment of a court and is executory after 10 days from the date of settlement unless repudiated within that period. If one party fails to comply, the other party may file a motion for execution with the Lupon or the proper court.

instruction: Ano ang Amicable Settlement at may bisa ba ito sa batas?
output: Ang Amicable Settlement ay isang nakasulat na kasunduan na pinirmahan ng magkabilang panig sa panahon ng barangay mediation o conciliation. Sa ilalim ng Revised Katarungang Pambarangay Law, ito ay may bisa at epekto ng final judgment ng korte at naipapatupad pagkalipas ng 10 araw mula sa petsa ng kasunduan maliban kung ito ay repudiated sa loob ng panahong iyon. Kung hindi sumunod ang isang partido, maaaring mag-file ng motion for execution sa Lupon o sa tamang korte.

instruction: When is a Certificate to File Action issued?
output: A Certificate to File Action (CFA) is issued by the Lupon Secretary or Pangkat Chairman when: (1) mediation and conciliation both fail to settle the dispute; (2) the respondent fails to appear at the mediation or conciliation hearing without valid reason; or (3) the case is not settled within the required period (15 days for mediation, 15 days for conciliation). The CFA is a mandatory requirement before filing the case in court for disputes covered by the Katarungang Pambarangay Law.

instruction: Kailan isinusulat ang Certificate to File Action?
output: Ang Certificate to File Action (CFA) ay ibinibigay ng Lupon Secretary o Pangkat Chairman kapag: (1) parehong nabigo ang mediation at conciliation; (2) hindi dumalo ang respondent sa hearing nang walang sapat na dahilan; o (3) hindi naresolba ang kaso sa loob ng takdang panahon (15 araw para sa mediation, 15 araw para sa conciliation). Ang CFA ay kinakailangan bago makapagsampa ng kaso sa korte para sa mga disputang sakop ng Katarungang Pambarangay Law.

instruction: What cases are exempt from barangay conciliation?
output: The following cases are exempt from barangay conciliation: (1) cases where one party is the government or its subdivisions; (2) cases where one party is a public officer or employee involving the exercise of official functions; (3) offenses punishable by imprisonment exceeding one year or a fine exceeding five thousand pesos; (4) cases where parties reside in different cities or municipalities; (5) cases involving real property located in different municipalities; (6) disputes involving parties who actually reside in barangays of different cities or municipalities; (7) cases requiring urgent legal action like habeas corpus or injunction; and (8) any case that may be barred by the statute of limitations.

instruction: Anong mga kaso ang hindi na kailangang dumaan sa barangay conciliation?
output: Ang mga sumusunod na kaso ay exempted sa barangay conciliation: (1) kaso kung saan ang isang partido ay ang gobyerno; (2) kaso laban sa public officer na may kinalaman sa kanyang opisyal na tungkulin; (3) mga kaso na may parusang higit sa isang taon na pagkabilanggo o multang higit sa limang libong piso; (4) mga partido na naninirahan sa magkaibang lungsod o munisipalidad; (5) kaso tungkol sa real property na nasa magkaibang munisipalidad; (6) mga kaso na nangangailangan ng agarang legal na aksyon tulad ng habeas corpus; at (7) mga kasong maaaring ma-bar ng statute of limitations.

instruction: My neighbor built a wall that blocks my only entrance. What should I do?
output: This is a property boundary dispute that can be mediated at the barangay level. Step 1: Go to your Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, a written complaint, and if available, your land title or tax declaration showing your property boundaries. Step 2: The Punong Barangay will summon your neighbor for mediation. Step 3: During mediation, both parties may present evidence such as land titles, tax declarations, or surveys. Step 4: The Punong Barangay may suggest a compromise such as creating an alternative passage or adjusting the wall. Step 5: If mediation fails, the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo may handle the conciliation. Step 6: If no agreement is reached, a Certificate to File Action will be issued so you may pursue the matter in court.

instruction: Nagtayo ng pader ang kapitbahay ko na humaharang sa aking daanan. Ano ang gagawin ko?
output: Ito ay isang property boundary dispute na maaaring ayusin sa barangay level. Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, sulat-reklamo, at kung mayroon, land title o tax declaration na nagpapakita ng hangganan ng iyong lupa. Hakbang 2: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa iyong kapitbahay para sa mediation. Hakbang 3: Sa mediation, maaaring magpakita ng ebidensya tulad ng land title, tax declaration, o survey. Hakbang 4: Maaaring magmungkahi ang Punong Barangay ng kompromiso tulad ng alternatibong daanan. Hakbang 5: Kung mabigo ang mediation, maaaring ituloy sa Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. Hakbang 6: Kung hindi pa rin magkasundo, mag-iisyu ng Certificate to File Action para makapagsampa sa korte.

instruction: How do I file a formal noise complaint against my neighbor at the barangay?
output: Step 1: Document the noise disturbances by noting dates, times, and duration. If possible, take video or audio recordings as evidence. Step 2: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, your written complaint, and any evidence you have gathered. Step 3: The Barangay Secretary will log your complaint. Step 4: The Punong Barangay will summon your neighbor for mediation. Step 5: During mediation, the Punong Barangay may propose solutions such as setting quiet hours or reducing noise levels. Step 6: If both parties agree, an Amicable Settlement will be signed. Step 7: If mediation fails, the matter may proceed to the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo for conciliation.

instruction: Paano mag-file ng formal na reklamo sa ingay ng kapitbahay sa barangay?
output: Hakbang 1: I-dokumento ang ingay sa pamamagitan ng pag-note ng mga petsa, oras, at tagal. Kung maaari, kumuha ng video o audio recording bilang ebidensya. Hakbang 2: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, sulat-reklamo, at mga ebidensya. Hakbang 3: Ire-record ng Barangay Secretary ang iyong reklamo. Hakbang 4: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa iyong kapitbahay para sa mediation. Hakbang 5: Sa mediation, maaaring magmungkahi ang Punong Barangay ng solusyon tulad ng quiet hours o pagbawas ng ingay. Hakbang 6: Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement. Hakbang 7: Kung mabigo ang mediation, maaaring ituloy sa Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo.

instruction: My neighbor owes me money and refuses to pay. How can the barangay help?
output: Unpaid debts between residents of the same barangay or city are subject to barangay conciliation. Step 1: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, a written complaint, and evidence of the debt such as promissory notes, receipts, text messages, bank transfer records, or witness statements. Step 2: The Punong Barangay will summon your neighbor for mediation. Step 3: During mediation, both parties will discuss a payment arrangement. Step 4: If an agreement is reached, an Amicable Settlement specifying the payment schedule will be signed. This settlement is legally enforceable. Step 5: If the debtor refuses to comply with the settlement, you may file a motion for execution with the Lupon or the proper court. Step 6: If mediation fails, a Certificate to File Action will be issued for you to file a small claims case in court.

instruction: May utang sa akin ang kapitbahay ko at ayaw magbayad. Paano makakatulong ang barangay?
output: Ang mga utang sa pagitan ng mga residente ng parehong barangay o lungsod ay sakop ng barangay conciliation. Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, sulat-reklamo, at ebidensya ng utang tulad ng promissory note, resibo, text messages, bank transfer records, o witness statements. Hakbang 2: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa iyong kapitbahay para sa mediation. Hakbang 3: Sa mediation, pag-uusapan ang payment arrangement. Hakbang 4: Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement na may payment schedule. Ito ay legally enforceable. Hakbang 5: Kung hindi sumunod ang may utang, maaaring mag-file ng motion for execution sa Lupon o korte. Hakbang 6: Kung mabigo ang mediation, mag-iisyu ng Certificate to File Action para makapagsampa ng small claims case sa korte.

instruction: My neighbor slapped me during an argument. Can the barangay handle this?
output: Slight physical injuries and minor altercations between residents of the same barangay are covered by barangay conciliation. Step 1: If you need medical attention, go to the nearest health center first and obtain a medical certificate documenting your injuries. Step 2: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, a written complaint, the medical certificate, and photos of your injuries if any. Step 3: The Punong Barangay will summon the respondent for mediation. Step 4: During mediation, the parties may agree on an apology, reimbursement of medical expenses, or other terms. Step 5: If settled, an Amicable Settlement will be signed. Step 6: If mediation fails, the case may proceed to the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo or you may be issued a Certificate to File Action. Note: If the injuries are serious or life-threatening, you may report directly to the police station.

instruction: Sinampal ako ng kapitbahay ko habang nag-aaway kami. Kaya ba itong ayusin sa barangay?
output: Ang slight physical injuries at minor na alitan sa pagitan ng mga residente ng parehong barangay ay sakop ng barangay conciliation. Hakbang 1: Kung kailangan mo ng medical attention, pumunta muna sa pinakamalapit na health center at kumuha ng medical certificate. Hakbang 2: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, sulat-reklamo, medical certificate, at larawan ng sugat kung mayroon. Hakbang 3: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa respondent para sa mediation. Hakbang 4: Sa mediation, maaaring magkasundo sa apology, pagbayad ng medical expenses, o iba pang kondisyon. Hakbang 5: Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement. Hakbang 6: Kung mabigo, maaaring ituloy sa Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo o mag-isyu ng Certificate to File Action. Tandaan: Kung malubha ang pinsala, maaaring direktang mag-report sa police station.

instruction: How do I resolve a property boundary dispute with my neighbor through the barangay?
output: Property boundary disputes between residents are subject to barangay conciliation. Step 1: Gather your evidence including land title, tax declaration, lot plan or survey, and any photos showing the current boundary. Step 2: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, a written complaint, and all your evidence. Step 3: The Punong Barangay will summon your neighbor for mediation. Step 4: During mediation, both parties may present their respective documents. The Punong Barangay may suggest a joint survey or compromise on the boundary. Step 5: If agreed, an Amicable Settlement will be signed and is legally binding. Step 6: If mediation fails, the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo will handle conciliation. Step 7: If still unresolved, a Certificate to File Action will be issued for court filing. A geodetic survey may be recommended to establish the exact boundaries.

instruction: Paano maresolba ang alitan sa hangganan ng lupa sa barangay?
output: Ang alitan sa hangganan ng lupa sa pagitan ng mga residente ay sakop ng barangay conciliation. Hakbang 1: Ihanda ang mga ebidensya tulad ng land title, tax declaration, lot plan o survey, at larawan ng kasalukuyang hangganan. Hakbang 2: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, sulat-reklamo, at lahat ng ebidensya. Hakbang 3: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa iyong kapitbahay para sa mediation. Hakbang 4: Sa mediation, maaaring magpakita ng kani-kanilang mga dokumento. Maaaring magmungkahi ang Punong Barangay ng joint survey o kompromiso. Hakbang 5: Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement na may legal na bisa. Hakbang 6: Kung mabigo, itutuloy sa Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. Hakbang 7: Kung hindi pa rin maayos, mag-iisyu ng Certificate to File Action. Maaaring irekomenda ang geodetic survey upang matukoy ang eksaktong hangganan.

instruction: How do I get a Barangay Clearance?
output: To obtain a Barangay Clearance, follow these steps: Step 1: Go to your Barangay Hall during office hours. Step 2: Bring the following requirements: valid government-issued ID, proof of residence (utility bill or barangay certificate of residency), and 1x1 or 2x2 ID photo (requirements may vary per barangay). Step 3: Fill out the application form provided by the Barangay Secretary. Step 4: Pay the processing fee (varies per barangay (ask your Barangay Hall for the current schedule of fees)). Step 5: Wait for the clearance to be processed, usually within the same day or the next business day. Step 6: The Punong Barangay or an authorized officer will sign the clearance. The Barangay Clearance is commonly required for employment, business permits, and other government transactions.

instruction: Paano kumuha ng Barangay Clearance?
output: Upang makakuha ng Barangay Clearance, sundin ang mga hakbang na ito: Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa iyong Barangay Hall sa oras ng opisina. Hakbang 2: Magdala ng mga sumusunod: valid government-issued ID, proof of residence (utility bill o barangay certificate of residency), at 1x1 o 2x2 ID photo (maaaring mag-iba ang requirements sa bawat barangay). Hakbang 3: Sagutan ang application form na ibibigay ng Barangay Secretary. Hakbang 4: Magbayad ng processing fee (nag-iiba sa bawat barangay (magtanong sa Barangay Hall para sa kasalukuyang schedule of fees)). Hakbang 5: Hintayin ang clearance na karaniwang makukuha sa parehong araw o sa susunod na araw ng opisina. Hakbang 6: Pipirmahan ng Punong Barangay o authorized officer ang clearance. Ang Barangay Clearance ay karaniwang kailangan para sa employment, business permits, at iba pang government transactions.

instruction: How do I get a Certificate of Residency from the barangay?
output: To obtain a Certificate of Residency: Step 1: Visit your Barangay Hall during office hours. Step 2: Bring a valid government-issued ID showing your address, and a proof of residence such as a utility bill (electric, water), lease contract, or a sworn statement from your landlord. Step 3: Fill out the application form. Step 4: Pay the minimal processing fee. Step 5: The certificate is usually released on the same day. This certificate is often required for school enrollment, voter registration, government assistance applications, and other transactions requiring proof of address.

instruction: Paano kumuha ng Certificate of Residency sa barangay?
output: Upang makakuha ng Certificate of Residency: Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall sa oras ng opisina. Hakbang 2: Magdala ng valid government-issued ID na may address, at proof of residence tulad ng utility bill (kuryente, tubig), lease contract, o sworn statement mula sa iyong landlord. Hakbang 3: Sagutan ang application form. Hakbang 4: Magbayad ng minimal na processing fee. Hakbang 5: Ang certificate ay karaniwang makukuha sa parehong araw. Ang certificate na ito ay madalas kailangan para sa school enrollment, voter registration, government assistance applications, at iba pang transaksyon na nangangailangan ng proof of address.

instruction: What do I need to get a Barangay Business Clearance?
output: To obtain a Barangay Business Clearance: Step 1: Visit the Barangay Hall where your business is located. Step 2: Bring the following: valid ID of the business owner, proof of business address (lease contract or property title), DTI registration for sole proprietorship or SEC registration for corporation, and the previous year's business permit if renewing. Step 3: Fill out the business clearance application form. Step 4: Pay the processing fee, which varies per barangay but varies per barangay (inquire at the Barangay Hall for the current fee schedule). Step 5: Wait for processing, usually 1 to 3 business days. The Barangay Business Clearance is a prerequisite for obtaining a Mayor's Permit or Business Permit from the municipal or city hall.

instruction: Ano ang kailangan para makakuha ng Barangay Business Clearance?
output: Upang makakuha ng Barangay Business Clearance: Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall kung saan matatagpuan ang iyong negosyo. Hakbang 2: Magdala ng mga sumusunod: valid ID ng may-ari, proof of business address (lease contract o property title), DTI registration para sa sole proprietorship o SEC registration para sa corporation, at nakaraang business permit kung nagre-renew. Hakbang 3: Sagutan ang business clearance application form. Hakbang 4: Magbayad ng processing fee na nag-iiba sa bawat barangay (magtanong sa Barangay Hall para sa kasalukuyang fee schedule). Hakbang 5: Hintayin ang processing na karaniwang 1 hanggang 3 araw ng opisina. Ang Barangay Business Clearance ay kinakailangan bago makakuha ng Mayor's Permit o Business Permit mula sa municipal o city hall.

instruction: Can the barangay help with family disputes over inherited property?
output: Yes. Non-criminal family disputes over inherited property between relatives living in the same barangay or city may be mediated at the barangay level. Step 1: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, a written complaint, and any relevant documents such as the title of the property, tax declarations, the will if any, or death certificate of the deceased. Step 2: The Punong Barangay will summon the other party for mediation. Step 3: During mediation, both parties may present their claims and evidence. Step 4: If a settlement is reached, an Amicable Settlement will be signed and will be legally binding. Step 5: If mediation fails, the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo will handle conciliation. Step 6: If unresolved, a Certificate to File Action will be issued. Note: Complex estate matters may ultimately need court intervention, but barangay mediation should be attempted first.

instruction: Maaari bang tulungan ng barangay ang alitan ng pamilya tungkol sa namanang lupa?
output: Oo. Ang hindi kriminal na alitan ng pamilya tungkol sa mana na sa pagitan ng mga kamag-anak na naninirahan sa parehong barangay o lungsod ay maaaring i-mediate sa barangay. Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, sulat-reklamo, at mga kaugnay na dokumento tulad ng titulo ng lupa, tax declarations, testamento kung mayroon, o death certificate ng namatay. Hakbang 2: Mag-iisyu ng summons ang Punong Barangay sa kabilang partido. Hakbang 3: Sa mediation, maaaring magpakita ng mga claim at ebidensya. Hakbang 4: Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement na may legal na bisa. Hakbang 5: Kung mabigo, itutuloy sa Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. Hakbang 6: Kung hindi pa rin maayos, mag-iisyu ng Certificate to File Action. Tandaan: Ang komplikadong usapin sa mana ay maaaring kailangan ng korte, ngunit dapat munang subukan ang barangay mediation.

instruction: How can the barangay help with 4Ps (Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program) concerns?
output: The barangay can assist with 4Ps-related concerns in the following ways: For enrollment issues, visit the Barangay Hall and bring your valid ID, proof of residence, and any DSWD-related documents. The barangay can issue a Certificate of Residency or Certificate of Indigency needed for your 4Ps application. For complaints about delisted status or missed payouts, the barangay can help you prepare a letter to DSWD and provide supporting certifications. For updating personal information, the barangay can issue updated certificates. The Barangay Council may also coordinate with the Municipal Link or DSWD field office on behalf of beneficiaries. It is advisable to keep copies of all documents submitted.

instruction: Paano makakatulong ang barangay sa mga concern sa 4Ps (Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program)?
output: Ang barangay ay makakatulong sa mga 4Ps concern sa mga sumusunod na paraan: Para sa enrollment issues, pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, at mga dokumento mula sa DSWD. Ang barangay ay maaaring mag-isyu ng Certificate of Residency o Certificate of Indigency na kailangan para sa 4Ps application. Para sa mga reklamo tungkol sa pag-delist o missed payouts, makakatulong ang barangay sa paggawa ng sulat sa DSWD at magbibigay ng supporting certifications. Para sa pag-update ng personal information, maaaring mag-isyu ang barangay ng updated certificates. Ang Barangay Council ay maaari ding makipag-coordinate sa Municipal Link o DSWD field office para sa mga benepisyaryo. Maipapayo na magtabi ng kopya ng lahat ng inisumite na dokumento.

instruction: How do I get a Certificate of Indigency from the barangay?
output: To obtain a Certificate of Indigency: Step 1: Visit your Barangay Hall during office hours. Step 2: Bring a valid ID and proof of residence. Step 3: Explain the purpose of the certificate (e.g., for hospital bills, legal aid, educational assistance, or government program enrollment). Step 4: The Barangay Secretary or Punong Barangay will verify your residency and economic status. Step 5: Fill out the application form. Step 6: The certificate is usually issued on the same day. This certificate is commonly required for free medical assistance, public attorney services, scholarship applications, and government aid programs.

instruction: Paano kumuha ng Certificate of Indigency sa barangay?
output: Upang makakuha ng Certificate of Indigency: Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall sa oras ng opisina. Hakbang 2: Magdala ng valid ID at proof of residence. Hakbang 3: Ipaliwanag ang layunin ng certificate (hal., para sa hospital bills, legal aid, educational assistance, o government program enrollment). Hakbang 4: Ibe-verify ng Barangay Secretary o Punong Barangay ang iyong residency at economic status. Hakbang 5: Sagutan ang application form. Hakbang 6: Ang certificate ay karaniwang inilalabas sa parehong araw. Ang certificate na ito ay karaniwang kailangan para sa libreng medical assistance, public attorney services, scholarship applications, at government aid programs.

instruction: My neighbor is raising chickens and the smell is unbearable. What can the barangay do?
output: Nuisance complaints such as foul odors from livestock are common barangay-level disputes. Step 1: Go to the Barangay Hall and bring a valid ID, proof of residence, and a written complaint describing the nuisance, its frequency, and how it affects your household. Photos may be helpful. Step 2: The Punong Barangay will summon your neighbor for mediation. Step 3: During mediation, the Punong Barangay may suggest solutions such as relocating the chickens farther from your property, improved waste management, or reducing the number of animals. Step 4: If an agreement is reached, an Amicable Settlement will be signed. Step 5: If mediation fails, the matter may proceed to the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. Step 6: The barangay may also check whether local ordinances on animal-keeping are being violated and take appropriate action.

instruction: Nag-aalaga ng manok ang kapitbahay ko at hindi na makayanan ang amoy. Ano ang magagawa ng barangay?
output: Ang mga reklamo sa nuisance tulad ng mabahong amoy mula sa hayop ay karaniwang barangay-level na alitan. Hakbang 1: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, at sulat-reklamo na naglalarawan ng nuisance, dalas nito, at kung paano ito nakakaapekto sa iyong pamilya. Makakatulong ang mga larawan. Hakbang 2: Mag-iisyu ang Punong Barangay ng summons sa kapitbahay para sa mediation. Hakbang 3: Sa mediation, maaaring magmungkahi ang Punong Barangay ng solusyon tulad ng paglipat ng manukan palayo sa iyong property, mas maayos na waste management, o pagbawas ng bilang ng hayop. Hakbang 4: Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement. Hakbang 5: Kung mabigo, itutuloy sa Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. Hakbang 6: Maaari ding i-check ng barangay kung may nalalabag na local ordinance sa pag-aalaga ng hayop.

instruction: What is the difference between barangay mediation and small claims court?
output: Barangay mediation is a required first step for most disputes between residents of the same city or municipality. It aims to settle disputes amicably without going to court, through mediation by the Punong Barangay and conciliation by the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. It covers both civil disputes and minor criminal matters. Small claims court, on the other hand, is a simplified court procedure for money claims within the threshold set by the Supreme Court (check the latest Rules on Small Claims for the current amount). It does not require a lawyer. You must first obtain a Certificate to File Action from the barangay before filing a small claims case, unless the case is exempted. In small claims court, a judge decides the case based on evidence presented by both parties.

instruction: Ano ang pagkakaiba ng barangay mediation at small claims court?
output: Ang barangay mediation ay isang kinakailangang unang hakbang para sa karamihan ng alitan sa pagitan ng mga residente ng parehong lungsod o munisipalidad. Layunin nitong ayusin ang mga alitan nang mapayapa sa pamamagitan ng mediation ng Punong Barangay at conciliation ng Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo. Sakop nito ang civil disputes at minor criminal matters. Ang small claims court naman ay isang simplified na proseso sa korte para sa money claims na sa loob ng threshold na itinakda ng Supreme Court (suriin ang pinakabagong Rules on Small Claims para sa kasalukuyang halaga). Hindi kailangan ng abogado. Kailangan mong kumuha muna ng Certificate to File Action mula sa barangay bago mag-file ng small claims case, maliban kung exempted ang kaso. Sa small claims court, ang hukom ang magdedesisyon batay sa ebidensyang ipinresenta ng magkabilang panig.

instruction: Can I back out of a barangay settlement I already signed?
output: Under the Katarungang Pambarangay Law, you may repudiate an Amicable Settlement within 10 days from the date of settlement on the ground that your consent was vitiated by fraud, violence, or intimidation. To repudiate, you must file a written notice with the Lupon Chairman stating your reasons. If the repudiation is valid, the settlement will be set aside and the case may proceed to court. After the 10-day period, the settlement becomes final and executory with the force of a court judgment and can no longer be repudiated.

instruction: Maaari ko bang bawiin ang kasunduan sa barangay na pinirmahan ko na?
output: Sa ilalim ng Katarungang Pambarangay Law, maaari mong i-repudiate ang Amicable Settlement sa loob ng 10 araw mula sa petsa ng kasunduan kung ang iyong pahintulot ay nakuha sa pamamagitan ng pandaraya, karahasan, o pananakot. Upang mag-repudiate, kailangan mong mag-file ng nakasulat na notice sa Lupon Chairman na nagtatakda ng iyong mga dahilan. Kung valid ang repudiation, itatabi ang kasunduan at maaaring dalhin sa korte ang kaso. Pagkatapos ng 10-araw na panahon, ang kasunduan ay nagiging final at executory na may bisa ng court judgment at hindi na maaaring bawiin.

instruction: The other party is not following our barangay settlement. What can I do?
output: If the other party fails to comply with a duly executed Amicable Settlement, you have the following remedies: Option 1: File a motion for execution with the Lupon Tagapamayapa through the Punong Barangay. The Lupon may issue an order of execution directing compliance. Option 2: If the settlement involves a sum of money and the Lupon cannot enforce it, you may file a motion for execution with the proper Municipal Trial Court or Metropolitan Trial Court that has jurisdiction over the barangay. Bring a copy of the Amicable Settlement, proof that the 10-day repudiation period has lapsed, and evidence of non-compliance. The court may then issue a writ of execution to enforce the settlement.

instruction: Hindi sinusunod ng kabilang partido ang kasunduan sa barangay. Ano ang magagawa ko?
output: Kung hindi sumusunod ang kabilang partido sa Amicable Settlement, mayroon kang mga sumusunod na remedyo: Opsyon 1: Mag-file ng motion for execution sa Lupon Tagapamayapa sa pamamagitan ng Punong Barangay. Maaaring mag-isyu ang Lupon ng order of execution. Opsyon 2: Kung ang kasunduan ay may kinalaman sa pera at hindi maipatupad ng Lupon, maaari kang mag-file ng motion for execution sa tamang Municipal Trial Court o Metropolitan Trial Court. Magdala ng kopya ng Amicable Settlement, patunay na lumipas na ang 10-araw na repudiation period, at ebidensya ng hindi pagsunod. Maaaring mag-isyu ang korte ng writ of execution upang ipatupad ang kasunduan.

instruction: What is a barangay blotter and when should I use it?
output: A barangay blotter is an official logbook maintained by the Barangay Secretary where incidents, complaints, and requests for assistance are recorded. You should use it to: (1) document incidents such as disturbances, minor altercations, or suspicious activities in your area; (2) create an official record of a dispute before or instead of filing a formal complaint; (3) report lost or found items; (4) document any incident that may be needed as evidence later. To have an entry recorded, visit the Barangay Hall, bring your valid ID, and narrate the facts of the incident to the Barangay Secretary who will enter it in the blotter. You may request a certified copy of your blotter entry for your records.

instruction: Ano ang barangay blotter at kailan ito dapat gamitin?
output: Ang barangay blotter ay isang opisyal na logbook na pinapanatili ng Barangay Secretary kung saan nire-record ang mga insidente, reklamo, at mga hiling para sa tulong. Dapat itong gamitin upang: (1) i-dokumento ang mga insidente tulad ng gulo, minor na alitan, o kahina-hinalang aktibidad; (2) lumikha ng opisyal na record ng alitan bago o sa halip na mag-file ng pormal na reklamo; (3) mag-report ng nawawala o natagpuang gamit; (4) i-dokumento ang anumang insidente na maaaring kailanganin bilang ebidensya sa hinaharap. Upang mailagay sa blotter, pumunta sa Barangay Hall, magdala ng valid ID, at isalaysay ang mga pangyayari sa Barangay Secretary. Maaari kang humingi ng certified copy ng iyong blotter entry.

instruction: My husband hit me. Should I go to the barangay or the police?
output: Domestic violence is a serious matter covered by Republic Act No. 9262 (Anti-Violence Against Women and Their Children Act). While the barangay is often the first point of contact, domestic violence cases are exempted from the Katarungang Pambarangay process and should not be subject to mediation or settlement. Here is what you should do: Step 1: If you are in immediate danger, call 911 or go to the nearest police station. Step 2: You may go to the Barangay Hall to have the incident recorded in the blotter, but the barangay should not mediate or force you to settle with the offender. Step 3: You may request a Barangay Protection Order (BPO) from the Punong Barangay, which can prohibit the offender from threatening, harassing, or contacting you for up to 15 days. Step 4: You may also file for a Temporary Protection Order (TPO) or Permanent Protection Order (PPO) at the court. Step 5: Seek medical attention and obtain a medical certificate. Keep all evidence including photos, messages, and medical records.

instruction: Sinaktan ako ng asawa ko. Dapat ba akong pumunta sa barangay o sa pulis?
output: Ang domestic violence ay seryosong usapin na sakop ng Republic Act No. 9262 (Anti-Violence Against Women and Their Children Act). Bagama't ang barangay ay madalas na unang pupuntahan, ang domestic violence cases ay exempted sa Katarungang Pambarangay at hindi dapat i-mediate o pilitin ang settlement. Narito ang dapat gawin: Hakbang 1: Kung nasa panganib ka, tumawag sa 911 o pumunta sa pinakamalapit na police station. Hakbang 2: Maaari kang pumunta sa Barangay Hall para mailagay ang insidente sa blotter, ngunit hindi dapat pilitin ng barangay na makipagkasundo sa offender. Hakbang 3: Maaari kang humiling ng Barangay Protection Order (BPO) mula sa Punong Barangay, na nagbabawal sa offender na manakot, mang-harass, o makipag-ugnayan sa iyo nang hanggang 15 araw. Hakbang 4: Maaari ka ring mag-file ng Temporary Protection Order (TPO) o Permanent Protection Order (PPO) sa korte. Hakbang 5: Magpatingin sa doktor at kumuha ng medical certificate. Itago ang lahat ng ebidensya.

instruction: What is a Barangay Protection Order (BPO)?
output: A Barangay Protection Order (BPO) is an order issued by the Punong Barangay to protect victims of violence against women and their children under RA 9262. The BPO can prohibit the respondent from threatening, harassing, annoying, or contacting the victim, and from entering the victim's residence. It is effective for 15 days from issuance. To obtain a BPO, the victim or any family member may file a complaint at the Barangay Hall. The Punong Barangay is required to issue the BPO on the same day. No mediation or conciliation is required for VAWC cases. After the BPO, the victim may apply for a Temporary Protection Order (TPO) at the court for longer protection.

instruction: Ano ang Barangay Protection Order (BPO)?
output: Ang Barangay Protection Order (BPO) ay isang utos na ibinibigay ng Punong Barangay upang protektahan ang mga biktima ng karahasan laban sa kababaihan at kanilang mga anak sa ilalim ng RA 9262. Ang BPO ay maaaring ipagbawal sa respondent ang pagbabanta, pang-haharass, pang-aasar, o pakikipag-ugnayan sa biktima, at ang pagpasok sa tirahan ng biktima. Ito ay may bisa ng 15 araw mula sa pag-isyu. Upang makakuha ng BPO, ang biktima o sinumang miyembro ng pamilya ay maaaring maghain ng reklamo sa Barangay Hall. Ang Punong Barangay ay kinakailangang mag-isyu ng BPO sa parehong araw. Walang mediation o conciliation na kailangan para sa VAWC cases. Pagkatapos ng BPO, maaaring mag-apply ang biktima ng Temporary Protection Order (TPO) sa korte para sa mas matagal na proteksyon.

instruction: What is the Lupon Tagapamayapa?
output: The Lupon Tagapamayapa is the body constituted in every barangay to settle disputes under the Katarungang Pambarangay system. It is composed of the Punong Barangay as chairman and 10 to 20 members who are residents of the barangay. Members are appointed by the Punong Barangay. The Lupon's main functions include: (1) administering the Katarungang Pambarangay system; (2) conducting mediation through the Punong Barangay; (3) forming the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo for conciliation; (4) issuing Certificates to File Action when conciliation fails; and (5) enforcing Amicable Settlements through execution proceedings. The Lupon serves for a term of three years.

instruction: Ano ang Lupon Tagapamayapa?
output: Ang Lupon Tagapamayapa ay ang katawan na itinatatag sa bawat barangay upang lutasin ang mga alitan sa ilalim ng Katarungang Pambarangay. Ito ay binubuo ng Punong Barangay bilang chairman at 10 hanggang 20 miyembro na mga residente ng barangay. Ang mga miyembro ay itinalaga ng Punong Barangay. Ang mga pangunahing tungkulin ng Lupon ay: (1) pamamahala ng Katarungang Pambarangay system; (2) pagsasagawa ng mediation sa pamamagitan ng Punong Barangay; (3) pagbuo ng Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo para sa conciliation; (4) pag-iisyu ng Certificates to File Action kapag nabigo ang conciliation; at (5) pagpapatupad ng Amicable Settlements. Ang Lupon ay nagsisilbi ng tatlong taong termino.

instruction: Is there a fee for filing a complaint at the barangay?
output: Filing a complaint under the Katarungang Pambarangay system is generally free of charge. The system was designed to provide accessible justice to all community members regardless of financial status. Some barangays may charge minimal administrative fees for processing documents, but the mediation and conciliation services themselves should not have substantial costs. If you are asked to pay a significant amount, you may inquire about the legal basis for the fee or report it to the municipal or city government.

instruction: May bayad ba ang paghahain ng reklamo sa barangay?
output: Ang paghahain ng reklamo sa ilalim ng Katarungang Pambarangay system ay karaniwang libre. Ang sistema ay dinisenyo upang magbigay ng accessible na hustisya sa lahat ng miyembro ng komunidad anuman ang kanilang kalagayang pinansyal. Ang ilang barangay ay maaaring maningil ng minimal na administrative fees para sa pagproseso ng mga dokumento, ngunit ang serbisyo ng mediation at conciliation ay hindi dapat magkaroon ng malaking gastos. Kung hinihingan ka ng malaking halaga, maaari mong itanong ang legal na batayan ng bayarin o i-report ito sa municipal o city government.

instruction: My neighbor's karaoke is too loud every weekend. What documents do I need to file a complaint?
output: To file a noise complaint at the barangay, bring the following documents: (1) Valid government-issued ID (such as PhilSys ID, voter's ID, driver's license, or passport); (2) Proof of residence (utility bill, barangay certificate, or lease contract); (3) Written complaint letter addressed to the Punong Barangay stating the details of the noise disturbance including dates, times, frequency, and how it affects your household; (4) Any supporting evidence such as video or audio recordings with timestamps, or written statements from other affected neighbors. Once filed, the Barangay Secretary will record it and the Punong Barangay will schedule a mediation hearing, usually within a few days.

instruction: Masyadong malakas ang karaoke ng kapitbahay ko tuwing weekend. Ano ang mga kailangang dokumento para makapag-file ng reklamo?
output: Upang mag-file ng reklamo sa ingay sa barangay, magdala ng mga sumusunod: (1) Valid government-issued ID (tulad ng PhilSys ID, voter's ID, driver's license, o passport); (2) Proof of residence (utility bill, barangay certificate, o lease contract); (3) Sulat-reklamo na naka-address sa Punong Barangay na naglalaman ng mga detalye ng ingay kabilang ang mga petsa, oras, dalas, at kung paano ito nakakaapekto sa iyong pamilya; (4) Mga supporting evidence tulad ng video o audio recordings na may timestamps, o written statements mula sa ibang apektadong kapitbahay. Kapag na-file, ire-record ito ng Barangay Secretary at mag-iseschedule ang Punong Barangay ng mediation hearing, karaniwang sa loob ng ilang araw.

instruction: What if my complaint is against the Punong Barangay?
output: If your dispute involves the Punong Barangay as a party, the mediation shall not be conducted by the Punong Barangay due to conflict of interest. Instead, the dispute shall be mediated by the Sangguniang Barangay member designated by law or by the municipal or city mayor. You may file your complaint directly with the Office of the Mayor or the Sangguniang Bayan/Panlungsod. Bring a valid ID, proof of residence, and a written complaint. The proper authority will arrange mediation or refer the matter to the appropriate body.

instruction: Paano kung ang reklamo ko ay laban sa Punong Barangay?
output: Kung ang alitan ay kinasasangkutan ng Punong Barangay bilang partido, hindi siya ang magsasagawa ng mediation dahil sa conflict of interest. Sa halip, ang mediation ay isasagawa ng miyembro ng Sangguniang Barangay na itinalaga ng batas o ng municipal o city mayor. Maaari mong i-file ang reklamo sa Office of the Mayor o sa Sangguniang Bayan/Panlungsod. Magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, at sulat-reklamo. Ang tamang awtoridad ang mag-aayos ng mediation o irerefer sa tamang katawan.

instruction: What is the role of the Barangay Tanod in dispute resolution?
output: The Barangay Tanod serves as the barangay's peacekeeping force and assists in maintaining order in the community. In the context of dispute resolution, Tanods may: (1) respond to disturbances and help calm the situation before formal mediation; (2) serve summons to parties involved in a barangay complaint; (3) assist the Punong Barangay during mediation hearings by maintaining order; (4) help enforce Amicable Settlements if needed; and (5) accompany parties or escort individuals if there is a risk of escalation. Tanods do not have the authority to arrest, but they can apprehend individuals caught in the act of committing a crime and turn them over to the police.

instruction: Ano ang papel ng Barangay Tanod sa paglutas ng alitan?
output: Ang Barangay Tanod ay nagsisilbing peacekeeping force ng barangay at tumutulong sa pagpapanatili ng kaayusan sa komunidad. Sa konteksto ng paglutas ng alitan, ang mga Tanod ay maaaring: (1) tumugon sa mga gulo at tumulong na pakalmahin ang sitwasyon bago ang pormal na mediation; (2) magsilbi ng summons sa mga partido sa barangay complaint; (3) tulungan ang Punong Barangay sa panahon ng mediation hearings sa pamamagitan ng pagpapanatili ng kaayusan; (4) tumulong sa pagpapatupad ng Amicable Settlements kung kinakailangan; at (5) samahan ang mga partido kung may panganib na lumala ang sitwasyon. Ang mga Tanod ay walang kapangyarihang mag-aresto, ngunit maaari nilang dakpin ang mga nahuhuli sa akto ng paggawa ng krimen at i-turnover sa pulis.

instruction: How long does the entire barangay conciliation process take?
output: The entire barangay conciliation process has the following timeframes under the law: (1) Mediation by the Punong Barangay must be completed within 15 days from the first hearing. (2) If mediation fails, the Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo must be constituted and must complete conciliation within 15 days from its constitution. (3) In total, the process should take no more than 30 days from the first mediation hearing. If no settlement is reached within these periods, the barangay must issue a Certificate to File Action. Note that in practice, scheduling may cause slight delays, but the legal timeframes should generally be followed.

instruction: Gaano katagal ang buong proseso ng barangay conciliation?
output: Ang buong proseso ng barangay conciliation ay may mga sumusunod na takdang panahon ayon sa batas: (1) Ang mediation ng Punong Barangay ay dapat makumpleto sa loob ng 15 araw mula sa unang hearing. (2) Kung mabigo ang mediation, ang Pangkat ng Tagapagkasundo ay dapat mabuo at ang conciliation ay dapat makumpleto sa loob ng 15 araw mula sa pagkakabuo nito. (3) Sa kabuuan, ang proseso ay hindi dapat lumampas sa 30 araw mula sa unang mediation hearing. Kung walang maabot na kasunduan sa loob ng mga panahong ito, ang barangay ay dapat mag-isyu ng Certificate to File Action. Sa aktwal, ang scheduling ay maaaring magdulot ng bahagyang pagkaantala, ngunit ang legal na timeframes ay dapat sundin.

instruction: Someone robbed my house. Should I go to the barangay?
output: Robbery is a serious criminal offense that is beyond the jurisdiction of the Katarungang Pambarangay. You should take the following steps: Step 1: Call 911 or go directly to the nearest police station to file a report. Step 2: Do not touch or move anything at the crime scene as this may be needed as evidence. Step 3: List the items stolen and provide descriptions. Step 4: Identify any witnesses and provide their contact information to the police. Step 5: You may also have the incident recorded in the Barangay Blotter for documentation purposes. The barangay cannot mediate robbery cases, but the Barangay Tanod or officials can help maintain order and coordinate with the police.

instruction: Ninakawan ang bahay namin. Dapat ba akong pumunta sa barangay?
output: Ang robbery ay isang seryosong kriminal na kaso na lampas sa hurisdiksyon ng Katarungang Pambarangay. Sundin ang mga hakbang na ito: Hakbang 1: Tumawag sa 911 o pumunta agad sa pinakamalapit na police station para mag-file ng report. Hakbang 2: Huwag hawakan o ilipat ang anumang bagay sa crime scene dahil maaaring kailanganin ito bilang ebidensya. Hakbang 3: Ilista ang mga nawalang gamit at magbigay ng paglalarawan. Hakbang 4: Tukuyin ang mga saksi at ibigay ang kanilang contact information sa pulis. Hakbang 5: Maaari mo ring ipalagay ang insidente sa Barangay Blotter para sa dokumentasyon. Hindi maaaring i-mediate ng barangay ang robbery cases, ngunit ang Barangay Tanod at mga opisyal ay makakatulong sa pagpapanatili ng kaayusan at pakikipagtulungan sa pulis.

instruction: What should I bring when attending a barangay mediation hearing?
output: When attending a barangay mediation hearing, whether as complainant or respondent, you should bring: (1) Valid government-issued ID; (2) Your copy of the complaint or the summons you received; (3) All relevant evidence supporting your position such as photos, videos, receipts, contracts, text messages, medical certificates, or any written documents; (4) A list of any witnesses who can support your claim, along with their contact details; (5) A clear outline of what resolution you would find acceptable. During the hearing, remain calm and respectful. The Punong Barangay will give both parties a chance to speak. Be honest and concise in presenting your side. You are not required to have a lawyer for barangay proceedings.

instruction: Ano ang dapat dalhin kapag dadalo sa barangay mediation hearing?
output: Kapag dadalo sa barangay mediation hearing, bilang complainant man o respondent, dapat dalhin ang: (1) Valid government-issued ID; (2) Kopya ng reklamo o ng summons na natanggap mo; (3) Lahat ng ebidensyang sumusuporta sa iyong panig tulad ng mga larawan, video, resibo, kontrata, text messages, medical certificates, o anumang nakasulat na dokumento; (4) Listahan ng mga saksi na maaaring sumuporta sa iyong claim kasama ang kanilang contact details; (5) Malinaw na outline ng kung anong resolusyon ang katanggap-tanggap sa iyo. Sa panahon ng hearing, manatiling kalmado at magalang. Bibigyan ng Punong Barangay ang parehong partido ng pagkakataong magsalita. Maging tapat at maikli sa pagpapahayag ng iyong panig. Hindi kailangan ng abogado sa barangay proceedings.

instruction: I am a senior citizen with a dispute. Does the barangay give any special accommodation?
output: Yes. Senior citizens and persons with disabilities (PWDs) are entitled to priority service at the Barangay Hall under Republic Act No. 9994 (Expanded Senior Citizens Act) and RA 7277 (Magna Carta for Persons with Disability). You should be given priority in scheduling and assistance. If you have difficulty going to the Barangay Hall, you may request through a family member or representative that the mediation be conducted at a more accessible location. Bring your valid ID and Senior Citizen ID or PWD ID. The barangay should provide reasonable accommodations to ensure you can participate fully in the mediation process.

instruction: Senior citizen ako na may alitan. May espesyal bang tulong ang barangay?
output: Oo. Ang mga senior citizen at persons with disabilities (PWD) ay may karapatan sa priority service sa Barangay Hall sa ilalim ng Republic Act No. 9994 (Expanded Senior Citizens Act) at RA 7277 (Magna Carta for Persons with Disability). Dapat kang bigyan ng priority sa scheduling at tulong. Kung nahihirapan kang pumunta sa Barangay Hall, maaari kang humiling sa pamamagitan ng isang miyembro ng pamilya o kinatawan na ang mediation ay isagawa sa mas accessible na lugar. Magdala ng valid ID at Senior Citizen ID o PWD ID. Dapat magbigay ang barangay ng reasonable accommodations upang matiyak na makalahok ka nang buo sa mediation process.

instruction: Can I file a complaint at the barangay on behalf of someone else?
output: Yes. You may file a barangay complaint on behalf of a minor child, an elderly family member, or a person who is physically unable to go to the Barangay Hall. Bring your own valid ID, the complainant's valid ID or a photocopy, a Special Power of Attorney (SPA) or written authorization from the actual complainant, and the written complaint. For minors, a parent or legal guardian may file the complaint. The Punong Barangay may require the actual complainant to appear during mediation hearings if their presence is necessary.

instruction: Pwede ba akong mag-file ng reklamo sa barangay para sa ibang tao?
output: Oo. Maaari kang mag-file ng barangay complaint para sa isang menor de edad, matandang miyembro ng pamilya, o taong hindi kayang pumunta sa Barangay Hall. Magdala ng iyong valid ID, valid ID o photocopy ng actual complainant, Special Power of Attorney (SPA) o nakasulat na authorization mula sa actual complainant, at ang sulat-reklamo. Para sa mga menor de edad, ang magulang o legal guardian ang maaaring mag-file. Maaaring hilinging ng Punong Barangay na dumalo ang actual complainant sa mediation hearings kung kinakailangan ang kanilang presensya.

instruction: What if both parties are from different barangays in the same city?
output: If the parties reside in different barangays within the same city or municipality, the complaint shall be filed in the barangay where the respondent actually resides. The conciliation process will be conducted by the Lupon Tagapamayapa of that barangay. Both parties must attend the mediation hearings at the respondent's barangay. Bring a valid ID, proof of your residence, and the written complaint.

instruction: Paano kung magkaibang barangay kami ng respondent pero pareho ng lungsod?
output: Kung ang mga partido ay naninirahan sa magkaibang barangay sa parehong lungsod o munisipalidad, ang reklamo ay dapat i-file sa barangay kung saan naninirahan ang respondent. Ang conciliation process ay isasagawa ng Lupon Tagapamayapa ng barangay na iyon. Dapat dumalo ang parehong partido sa mediation hearings sa barangay ng respondent. Magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, at sulat-reklamo.

instruction: Can I bring a lawyer to barangay mediation?
output: Under the Katarungang Pambarangay Law, parties are generally not allowed to be assisted by lawyers during mediation and conciliation proceedings. The purpose is to keep the process informal, accessible, and community-based. However, a party may consult a lawyer before or after the hearing for advice. If a party feels the need for legal representation due to the complexity of the matter, this should be raised with the Punong Barangay. In practice, the process is designed so that lawyers are not necessary, and the Punong Barangay or Pangkat members guide both parties through the process.

instruction: Pwede ba akong magdala ng abogado sa barangay mediation?
output: Sa ilalim ng Katarungang Pambarangay Law, ang mga partido ay karaniwang hindi pinapayagang tulungan ng abogado sa panahon ng mediation at conciliation proceedings. Ang layunin ay panatilihing impormal, accessible, at community-based ang proseso. Gayunpaman, maaaring kumonsulta ang isang partido sa abogado bago o pagkatapos ng hearing. Kung nararamdaman ng isang partido na kailangan ang legal na representasyon dahil sa pagiging komplikado ng usapin, dapat itong ipaabot sa Punong Barangay. Sa aktwal, ang proseso ay dinisenyo upang hindi kailangan ng abogado, at ang Punong Barangay o mga miyembro ng Pangkat ang gagabay sa magkabilang partido.

instruction: My neighbor is selling drugs. Should I report this to the barangay?
output: Drug offenses are serious criminal matters that are not subject to barangay mediation. Do not attempt to resolve this at the barangay level. Step 1: Report the activity directly to the nearest police station, the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA), or call 911. Step 2: You may also report anonymously through the PDEA hotline. Step 3: For documentation purposes, you may also have the information recorded in the Barangay Blotter, but the barangay should refer the matter to law enforcement. Step 4: Provide as many details as possible including dates, times, and descriptions of the activity. Do not confront the suspected drug dealer yourself.

instruction: Nagbebenta ng droga ang kapitbahay ko. Dapat ba itong i-report sa barangay?
output: Ang mga kasong may kinalaman sa droga ay seryosong kriminal na usapin na hindi sakop ng barangay mediation. Huwag subukang ayusin ito sa barangay level. Hakbang 1: I-report ang aktibidad direkta sa pinakamalapit na police station, Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA), o tumawag sa 911. Hakbang 2: Maaari ka ring mag-report nang anonymous sa pamamagitan ng PDEA hotline. Hakbang 3: Para sa dokumentasyon, maaari mo ring ipalagay ang impormasyon sa Barangay Blotter, ngunit dapat i-refer ng barangay ang usapin sa law enforcement. Hakbang 4: Magbigay ng maraming detalye hangga't maaari kabilang ang mga petsa, oras, at paglalarawan ng aktibidad. Huwag harapin ang suspetsadong drug dealer.

instruction: How are barangay ordinances different from barangay resolutions?
output: Barangay ordinances and resolutions are both enacted by the Sangguniang Barangay but serve different purposes. An ordinance is a local law that prescribes rules of conduct, imposes penalties for violations, and is generally permanent. Examples include anti-noise ordinances, curfew ordinances, and waste management ordinances. A resolution, on the other hand, is a formal expression of opinion or intent by the Sangguniang Barangay. It does not have the force of law and is used for matters such as requesting assistance, endorsing projects, or expressing congratulations. If you want to know what rules apply in your barangay, ask for copies of the enacted ordinances at the Barangay Hall.

instruction: Ano ang pagkakaiba ng barangay ordinance at barangay resolution?
output: Ang barangay ordinance at resolution ay parehong ginagawa ng Sangguniang Barangay ngunit may magkaibang layunin. Ang ordinance ay isang local na batas na nagtatakda ng mga panuntunan sa pag-uugali, nagpapataw ng parusa sa mga paglabag, at karaniwang permanente. Mga halimbawa nito ay anti-noise ordinance, curfew ordinance, at waste management ordinance. Ang resolution naman ay isang pormal na pagpapahayag ng opinyon o intensyon ng Sangguniang Barangay. Wala itong bisa ng batas at ginagamit para sa mga bagay tulad ng paghiling ng tulong, pag-endorse ng mga proyekto, o pagpapahayag ng pagbati. Kung gusto mong malaman ang mga patakaran sa iyong barangay, humingi ng mga kopya ng mga naisagawang ordinance sa Barangay Hall.

instruction: What happens if I file a court case without going to the barangay first?
output: For disputes covered by the Katarungang Pambarangay Law, filing a case in court without first going through barangay conciliation may result in the case being dismissed. The court may dismiss the complaint on the ground of prematurity or lack of compliance with the barangay conciliation requirement. This is a condition precedent that must be satisfied before the court can take jurisdiction. The defendant or respondent may raise the lack of a Certificate to File Action as a defense. If your case is dismissed for this reason, you will need to go through the barangay process first and obtain a Certificate to File Action before refiling the case. However, cases exempted from barangay conciliation are not affected by this requirement.

instruction: Ano ang mangyayari kung mag-file ako ng kaso sa korte nang hindi muna dumaan sa barangay?
output: Para sa mga alitang sakop ng Katarungang Pambarangay Law, ang pag-file ng kaso sa korte nang hindi muna dumaan sa barangay conciliation ay maaaring magresulta sa pagbasura ng kaso. Maaaring i-dismiss ng korte ang reklamo sa dahilang prematurity o kawalan ng compliance sa barangay conciliation requirement. Ito ay isang condition precedent na dapat matugunan bago magkaroon ng jurisdiction ang korte. Ang defendant ay maaaring gamitin ang kawalan ng Certificate to File Action bilang depensa. Kung ma-dismiss ang kaso sa dahilang ito, kailangan mong dumaan muna sa barangay process at kumuha ng Certificate to File Action bago muling i-file ang kaso. Gayunpaman, ang mga kasong exempted sa barangay conciliation ay hindi apektado ng requirement na ito.

instruction: My neighbor constantly shouts insults at me whenever I pass by. Can the barangay help?
output: Yes, repeated verbal abuse between residents is a dispute that may be mediated at the barangay. File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall with your valid ID, proof of residence, and any evidence such as witness statements or recordings. The Punong Barangay will summon both parties for mediation to agree on stopping the behavior.

instruction: A group of teenagers in our area verbally bully my child every day. What should I do?
output: File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall and bring your valid ID, proof of residence, and a detailed account of the incidents including dates, times, and names of those involved. The Punong Barangay will call the parents or guardians of the teenagers for mediation to address the behavior and establish preventive measures.

instruction: Someone in my community keeps calling me offensive names in public. How do I file a complaint?
output: Go to the Barangay Hall and submit a written complaint describing the incidents, including dates, locations, and witnesses. Bring your valid ID and proof of residence. The Punong Barangay will schedule a mediation session to resolve the matter and agree on terms that prevent recurrence.

instruction: My child is being mocked and humiliated by an adult neighbor. What can I do at the barangay level?
output: File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall with your valid ID, proof of residence, and a description of the incidents including dates and any witnesses. The Punong Barangay will summon the adult neighbor for mediation, where an agreement to stop the behavior and a written apology may be arranged.

instruction: Can I file a barangay complaint if someone repeatedly shames me in front of other people?
output: Yes, public shaming that causes distress between residents of the same area may be mediated at the barangay. Bring a written complaint, valid ID, proof of residence, and any evidence such as witness statements. The Punong Barangay will facilitate mediation to address the conduct and prevent it from continuing.

instruction: What evidence do I need for a verbal bullying complaint at the barangay?
output: Prepare a written complaint describing each incident with dates, times, and locations. Supporting evidence may include witness statements from neighbors who observed the behavior, audio or video recordings if available, and any text messages or social media posts related to the bullying. Bring your valid ID and proof of residence when filing.

instruction: Lagi akong minamaliit at inaaway ng kapitbahay ko sa harap ng iba. Ano ang gagawin ko?
output: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo na may detalye ng mga insidente kasama ang petsa, oras, at mga saksi. Magdala ng valid ID at proof of residence. Ipapatawag ng Punong Barangay ang iyong kapitbahay para sa mediation upang mapigilan ang pang-aabuso at maabot ang isang kasunduan.

instruction: Palaging tinatawag ng masasamang salita ang anak ko ng mga kapitbahay. Saan ako magreklamo?
output: Maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo sa Barangay Hall at magdala ng valid ID, proof of residence, at listahan ng mga insidente kasama ang petsa at pangalan ng mga sangkot. Ipapatawag ng Punong Barangay ang mga magulang ng mga bata para sa mediation upang mapagkasunduan ang pagtigil ng verbal bullying.

instruction: Ano ang mga ebidensya na kailangan ko para sa reklamo ng verbal bullying sa barangay?
output: Ihanda ang nakasulat na reklamo na may detalye ng bawat insidente kasama ang petsa, oras, at lugar. Maaaring mag-submit ng witness statements mula sa mga nakasaksi, audio o video recordings kung mayroon, at mga text messages o social media posts na may kaugnayan. Magdala rin ng valid ID at proof of residence sa pag-file.

instruction: Maaari bang ireklamo sa barangay kung paulit-ulit akong pinapahiya ng kapitbahay sa publiko?
output: Oo, ang paulit-ulit na pampublikong pagpapahiya sa pagitan ng mga residente ay maaaring i-mediate sa barangay. Maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo sa Barangay Hall kasama ang valid ID, proof of residence, at ebidensya tulad ng witness statements. Itatakda ng Punong Barangay ang mediation upang matigil ang ganitong pag-uugali.

instruction: Paano ko ireklamo ang matandang kapitbahay na palaging nang-iinsulto ng anak ko?
output: Pumunta sa Barangay Hall at maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo na naglalaman ng mga detalye ng insidente kasama ang petsa, oras, at saksi. Magdala ng valid ID at proof of residence. Ipapatawag ng Punong Barangay ang respondent para sa mediation upang mapagkasunduan ang pagtigil ng pang-iinsulto at posibleng nakasulat na paumanhin.

instruction: Ano ang mangyayari sa mediation para sa verbal bullying sa barangay?
output: Sa mediation, parehong partido ang bibigyan ng pagkakataong magsalita sa harap ng Punong Barangay. Tatalakayin ang mga insidente at ang epekto nito sa biktima. Kung magkasundo, pipirmahan ang Amicable Settlement na maaaring maglaman ng pangako na itigil ang pang-aabuso, paumanhin, at mga hakbang upang maiwasan ang pag-ulit.

instruction: A neighbor's child keeps pushing and hitting my child. What can the barangay do?
output: File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall with your valid ID, proof of residence, a description of each incident, and any evidence such as photos of injuries or a medical certificate. The Punong Barangay will summon both sets of parents for mediation to agree on corrective measures and prevent further incidents.

instruction: My child came home with bruises from being bullied by another child in the community. Should I go to the barangay?
output: Yes, go to the nearest health center first and obtain a medical certificate documenting the injuries. Then file a written complaint at the Barangay Hall with your valid ID, proof of residence, the medical certificate, and photos of the bruises. The Punong Barangay will call both families for mediation to address accountability and agree on measures to prevent recurrence.

instruction: An older child in our area regularly intimidates and pushes smaller children. How do I report this?
output: File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall with your valid ID, proof of residence, and a detailed description of the incidents including dates and names of affected children. Witness statements from other parents or neighbors will strengthen the complaint. The Punong Barangay will summon the parents of the older child for mediation and agreement on corrective action.

instruction: What documents do I need to report minor physical bullying at the barangay?
output: Prepare the following: a written complaint describing the incidents with dates and times, your valid ID, proof of residence, a medical certificate if there are injuries, photos of any visible injuries, and written statements from witnesses. Submit all documents at the Barangay Hall and request that the complaint be logged in the Barangay Blotter.

instruction: Can the barangay mediate if a neighbor's child threw a stone at my child and caused a wound?
output: Yes, minor physical altercations involving children between families in the same barangay may be mediated. Have the injury treated first and obtain a medical certificate. File a written complaint at the Barangay Hall with your valid ID, proof of residence, the medical certificate, and photos. The Punong Barangay will call both families for mediation to settle the matter, which may include reimbursement of medical costs.

instruction: What happens if the parent of the bully refuses to attend barangay mediation?
output: If the respondent refuses to attend mediation without a valid reason after receiving a summons, the Punong Barangay may issue a Certificate to File Action. This document allows you to bring the case to the proper court. The non-appearance will also be noted in the barangay records as part of the documentation.

instruction: Lagi na lang tinutulak at sinasaktan ng anak ng kapitbahay ang anak ko. Ano ang gagawin ko?
output: Maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo sa Barangay Hall kasama ang valid ID, proof of residence, detalye ng bawat insidente, at ebidensya tulad ng larawan ng sugat o medical certificate. Ipapatawag ng Punong Barangay ang magulang ng dalawang bata para sa mediation upang magkasundo sa mga hakbang na pipigil sa pag-ulit.

instruction: Umuwi ang anak ko na may pasa dahil binully siya ng kapitbahay. Dapat ba akong pumunta sa barangay?
output: Oo, pumunta muna sa pinakamalapit na health center at kumuha ng medical certificate. Pagkatapos, maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo sa Barangay Hall kasama ang valid ID, proof of residence, medical certificate, at larawan ng pasa. Ipapatawag ng Punong Barangay ang parehong pamilya para sa mediation upang pag-usapan ang pananagutan at paraan ng pag-iwas sa pag-ulit.

instruction: May batang mas matanda na palaging nananakot at nanunulak sa mga maliliit na bata sa aming lugar. Paano ko ito ire-report?
output: Maghain ng nakasulat na reklamo sa Barangay Hall kasama ang valid ID, proof of residence, at detalyadong paglalarawan ng mga insidente kasama ang petsa at pangalan ng mga apektadong bata. Makakatulong ang witness statements mula sa ibang magulang. Ipapatawag ng Punong Barangay ang magulang ng batang nananakot para sa mediation at kasunduan sa corrective action.

instruction: Ano ang mga dokumento na kailangan para mag-report ng minor physical bullying sa barangay?
output: Ihanda ang sumusunod: nakasulat na reklamo na may petsa at detalye ng mga insidente, valid ID, proof of residence, medical certificate kung may sugat, larawan ng anumang pinsala, at witness statements. I-submit ang lahat sa Barangay Hall at hilingin na mailagay sa Barangay Blotter ang reklamo.
